title: centaurea hanryi subsp. shuttleworthii
intro: Centaurea hanryi subsp. shuttleworthii, also known as Shuttleworth's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Centaurea hanryi subsp. shuttleworthii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make poultices or topical applications to treat skin conditions or wounds.
